description: Registry of Tiger-Skills AI orchestrated processes.

skills.name: Skills Orchestrator Pipeline
skills.description: A runbook for reasoning models to execute the complete tiger-skills pipeline in the correct dependency order. Routes documents through different skill chains based on document type.

skills.name: Document Classification
skills.description: Classify financial PDFs by extracting company identity, document type, and date metadata. Validates ticker via Yahoo Finance with multi-step fallback.

skills.name: Document Organization
skills.description: Move processed documents from processing_data/ to output_data/TICKER/, create/update company metadata, and perform cross-document date healing.

skills.name: Financial Calculations
skills.description: Compute derived financial metrics (EBITA, tax rates, invested capital, ROIC) from previously extracted data. All calculations are pure math — no LLM calls needed.

skills.name: EBITA Calculation
skills.description: Calculate EBITA (Earnings Before Interest, Tax, and Amortization) from income statement and GAAP reconciliation data.

skills.name: Invested Capital Calculation
skills.description: Calculate Net Working Capital, Net Long-Term Operating Assets, and total Invested Capital from balance sheet data.

skills.name: Summary Table
skills.description: Compile all calculated metrics into a final summary table with NOPAT and ROIC.

skills.name: Tax Rate Calculation
skills.description: Calculate effective tax rate and adjusted (operating) tax rate from income statement and EBITA data.

skills.name: Financial Data Extraction
skills.description: Extract structured financial data from classified PDFs. Contains 5 sub-skills for balance sheet, income statement, shares outstanding, organic growth, and GAAP reconciliation.

skills.name: Balance Sheet Extraction
skills.description: Extract balance sheet line items from a financial PDF, standardize names via Tiger-Transformer, and validate calculations.

skills.name: GAAP Reconciliation Extraction
skills.description: Extract GAAP-to-non-GAAP operating income or EBITDA reconciliation tables from earnings announcements.

skills.name: Income Statement Extraction
skills.description: Extract income statement line items from a financial PDF, standardize names via Tiger-Transformer, and normalize expense signs.

skills.name: Organic Growth Extraction
skills.description: Extract organic/constant-currency growth percentage and prior-year revenue from a financial PDF.

skills.name: Shares Outstanding Extraction
skills.description: Extract basic and diluted shares outstanding from a financial PDF.

skills.name: Qualitative Assessment
skills.description: Assess economic moat, EBITA margin trajectory, and organic growth trajectory from analyst reports, transcripts, or articles. Produces structured outlook that feeds into DCF assumption generation.

skills.name: Financial Modeling
skills.description: Build a multi-stage DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) model from historical financial data and qualitative outlook assumptions. Produces WACC, 10-year projections, terminal value, enterprise value, and intrinsic value per share.

skills.name: DCF Assumptions
skills.description: Create the three-stage DCF assumptions (revenue growth, EBITA margin, marginal capital turnover) by blending historical trends with qualitative outlook.

skills.name: DCF Model
skills.description: Project 10 years of free cash flow using three-stage interpolated assumptions, calculate terminal value, and derive enterprise value via discounted cash flow.

skills.name: Intrinsic Value Calculation
skills.description: Convert Enterprise Value to equity value per share by subtracting debt, adding cash, and dividing by diluted shares outstanding.

skills.name: JSON Export
skills.description: Export the complete DCF model, assumptions, and intrinsic value into a machine-readable JSON file for the interactive financial model viewer.

skills.name: WACC Calculation
skills.description: Calculate Weighted Average Cost of Capital using CAPM for equity cost and market data for debt cost. WACC is the discount rate used in the DCF model.
